title: Game Port Reference Capture: Magic Tower
summary: Stand-in for the asset-capture step of a Flash-to-modern-engine port: drive the legacy Magic Tower (mota-24) build under Ruffle and collect per-floor reference screenshots that downstream engineers will use as ground-truth for tile layout, HUD placement, and level-script reconstruction.
taskPrompt:
## Context

A small game studio plans to re-implement the legacy Flash dungeon-crawler **Magic Tower (mota-24)** on a modern stack. Before any port code is written, the porting workflow requires a clean set of **per-floor reference screenshots** captured from the original build — these screenshots become the visual contract that downstream engineers diff against while re-creating tile maps, HUD elements, and per-floor enemy/item placement.

Your role in that workflow is the reference-capture pass: drive the original game far enough to expose floors 1–3, and save one labeled screenshot per floor reached.

## Goal

Launch the original Flash build under Ruffle, advance through the early-game progression, and produce labeled per-floor reference screenshots up to and including floor 3.

## Steps

1. The game is auto-opened by the task setup at `mota_24_ez\input\mota-24.swf` in Ruffle. If the window is not already focused, focus it.
2. Wait for the title / loading screens to finish and enter the running game world.
3. Play forward through floors 1 → 3 using standard movement and interaction (no cheats, no save-state hacks). Combat / item pickups / stair-use are expected; this is what a porting engineer would do to surface each floor.
4. Each time you arrive on a new floor, capture a full-window screenshot of the rendered game and save it to `mota_24_ez\output\$FLOOR_NUMBER$.png`, where `$FLOOR_NUMBER$` is the integer floor index (`1.png`, `2.png`, `3.png`).

## Acceptance

- One PNG per floor reached, named by floor number, exists in the output directory.
- Each PNG must depict the correct floor (visually matches the original game's floor layout) — these will be diffed against held-out reference frames.
agentMustDo: Focus the Ruffle window with mota-24.swf loaded (the task setup auto-launches it)
Wait for the title / loading screens and enter the actual game world
Play forward through floors 1 → 3 using normal movement and interaction (no cheats)
On arrival at each new floor, save a full-window screenshot as output/<floor>.png (1.png, 2.png, 3.png)
evaluation:
**Hard gate conditions:** Per-floor screenshot files (`<floor>.png`) must exist under `output/`.
**Scoring method:** Per-floor LLM-vision comparison via `evaluate_milestone_mode` + `compare_screenshots_game` — each captured floor screenshot is matched against a held-out reference frame of the same floor from the original build. Floors are scored independently and averaged; this mirrors the porting workflow where each floor's reference must independently match the original before reimplementation can proceed.
**What does a PERFECT output look like?** A complete set of per-floor reference frames (`1.png`, `2.png`, `3.png`) under `output/`, each visually matching its corresponding original floor — ready to be handed off as the port's visual ground truth.
**What does a WRONG/empty output look like?** Missing per-floor screenshots, mislabeled floor numbers, or captures from the wrong floor (e.g. all three screenshots from floor 1) — yields a partial or 0.0 score.

evaluatorCredentials.purpose: OpenAI vision/LLM judge scores the deliverable during evaluate(); sourced from secret/eval_time/openai.env
